theme: Choose cable rest-lengths to reach a target shape
title: Retensioning the Ground-Braced Mast Array
mutation: composite: the target shape is scored only if it is a STABLE equilibrium of the whole spring/strut network (force-balance-equilibrium + prestress-inversion), and the objective couples shape error to a prestress-magnitude cost (shape-target), so the intended construction must invert desired forces into stiffness-aware, minimal-but-sufficient cable tensions rather than matching lengths to the target.
strategies: trivial: halve every cable's target-shape gap, ignoring stiffness and force balance (the checker's own baseline)

naive greedy: set every cable's rest length to (target gap - fixed margin), tensioning every cable uniformly regardless of stiffness -- unbalanced on stiffness-mismatched pairs and over-taut/wasteful everywhere

insight (strong): match TENSIONS (not lengths) on each free node's opposing cable pair via r = gap - T/k with equal T on both sides, using the smallest tension that is unambiguously stable -- exact force balance, low prestress cost

general RL headroom: dial the shared tension T down per-node toward the minimal stable threshold (instead of a fixed cap) and route through the least-cost grounded neighbors, shaving the residual prestress-cost term further
